scaled_oracle_cases.description: Multi-line valid input; function must return a list of dictionaries, one per line.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function handles multiple lines and returns a list of dicts.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Output must be a list of dictionaries with keys: NLL, mu_normal, mu_tumors, C, p*. Each dictionary corresponds to one data line.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old gold solution returns a single dict or only parses the first line.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n12.5\t0.4,0.6\t2:3\t0.1,0.2\n13.1\t0.5,0.5\t2:2\t0.2,0.3\n"
fname = "theta_multi.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Modify the input file to contain multiple lines (each a separate tumor sample), requiring the function to parse all lines and return aggregated results.

Add a parameter to the function that toggles output format between single sample dict (default for one line) and list of dicts (for multiple lines). However, to make it simpler, change the required output to always be a list of dicts (even for one line). This increases constraint on output structure.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Malformed input: missing mu field should raise ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that function raises ValueError when a required field (mu) is missing.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: If any required field is missing or malformed, raise ValueError with an appropriate message.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old gold solution assigns None instead of raising exception.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n12.5\t\t2:3\t0.1,0.2\n"
fname = "theta_missing_mu.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
try:
    result = parse_theta_results(fname)
    print("No exception")
except ValueError as e:
    print("ValueError:", e)
finally:
    os.remove(fname)
covered_requirements: Add constraint that the function must raise a ValueError if any required field is missing or malformed (e.g., non-numeric NLL, missing mu, incorrect number of mu components).
